pots_identified.description: Yellow/gold colored pot, visible in EARLIEST and T-5. Contains dark soil and some organic debris.
Black pot, central to the observation. Contains soil, then a plant from T-3.
White plastic cup, introduced from T-1. Contains water and a plant fragment.

plants_identified.description: A young plant with rounded, dark green leaves, appearing in P2 from T-3.
A small green leaf/plant fragment, floating/submerged in the white cup from T-1.

scale_anchor_identified.description: White rabbit (5cm) in p3 pot, as per WORLD MODEL.

registry_baseline.P2: Mexican Mint (Black Pot | White Rabbit anchor)

reconciliation_findings.observed_status: Present throughout. Initially contained bare soil, then a plant from T-3.
Absent in EARLIEST, T-5 (bare soil). Appears as a young plant in T-3. Morphology consistent with a young mint.
reconciliation_findings.discrepancy: Declared 'Systemic Loss' in EARLIEST and T-5 due to absence. Its appearance in T-3 is a 'New Introduction/Intervention' relative to the prior state, though it aligns with the registry's expectation for P2's occupant.

reconciliation_findings.item: White Rabbit anchor (5cm in p3 pot)
White plastic cup with water and plant fragment

P2_Plant.observation: P2 contains dark, dry soil with some indistinct debris. No plant visible. The expected Mexican Mint is absent.
P2 contains dark, dry soil. Light-colored fragments, consistent with crushed eggshells, are now clearly visible on the soil surface. No plant visible. The expected Mexican Mint is absent.
P2_Plant.what_changed: Eggshell fragments are more distinct and prominent in P2. Soil remains dry.

P2_Plant.observation: Image is completely black. No visual information available for P2 or any other element.
P2_Plant.what_changed: Complete loss of visual data; camera feed interruption or malfunction.

P2_Plant.observation: A young plant with 4-5 rounded, dark green leaves is now present in the center of P2. The soil appears dark and moist. Eggshell fragments are still visible. The plant exhibits good turgor and healthy coloration.
P2_Plant.what_changed: A plant has been introduced into P2. The soil moisture has significantly increased. The yellow pot is no longer clearly visible. Electronic components, a blue book, and a white pen are now visible on the desk.

P2_Plant.observation: The plant in P2 shows slight growth, now presenting 5-6 rounded, dark green leaves. Leaves appear marginally larger and more robust. Soil remains dark and moist. Eggshell fragments are present. Overall health appears stable and good.
P2_Plant.what_changed: The plant in P2 has undergone minor growth, with slightly larger leaves and potentially one new leaf. The overall lighting appears brighter than T-3.

P2_Plant.observation: The plant in P2 maintains its healthy appearance with 5-6 rounded, dark green leaves. Leaf turgor is excellent. Soil remains dark and moist. Eggshell fragments are present. A white plastic cup containing water and a small green plant fragment is now positioned to the right of P2.
P2_Plant.what_changed: A new white plastic cup with water and a plant fragment has been introduced. The electronic components near P2 have shifted slightly. The plant in P2 remains stable.

P2_Plant.observation: The plant in P2 continues to appear healthy and stable, consistent with T-1. It has 5-6 rounded, dark green leaves with good turgor. Soil is dark and moist, with eggshell fragments. The white cup and its contents are unchanged.
P2_Plant.what_changed: Minimal change from T-1. The plant in P2 shows continued stability. The white cup and its contents are unchanged. Overall image appears slightly dimmer.

P_WhiteCup_Fragment.observation: A small green leaf/plant fragment is visible floating or submerged in the water within the white plastic cup.
P_WhiteCup_Fragment.what_changed: Introduction of the white cup and its contents.

P_WhiteCup_Fragment.observation: The small green leaf/plant fragment remains visible in the white plastic cup, appearing intact and green.

biome_observations.soil_texture_P2: Initially dry and somewhat compacted (EARLIEST, T-5), becoming consistently dark and moist from T-3 onwards. No visible cracking. Presence of eggshell fragments from T-5.
biome_observations.soil_texture_P_Yellow: Appears dry and somewhat compacted in EARLIEST and T-5.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No discernible weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings observed in any pot.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: No visible fungal growth or mold detected.
biome_observations.debris_desk_surface: Various wires and electronic components are present on the desk surface from T-3 onwards, with minor positional shifts. A blue book and white pen are visible in T-3. A white plastic cup is present from T-1.

temporal_deltas.change: Eggshell fragments become more distinct and prominent on the soil surface of P2. No other significant changes in plant or pot status.
Complete loss of visual data; the image is entirely black, indicating a camera feed interruption or malfunction.

A significant intervention: a young plant (inferred Mexican Mint) has been introduced into P2. The soil in P2 is now visibly moist. The yellow pot is no longer clearly in view. New desk items (electronic components, blue book, white pen) are visible.
The plant in P2 shows minor but discernible growth, with leaves appearing slightly larger and potentially one new leaf. Overall lighting appears brighter, enhancing visibility.
A white plastic cup containing water and a small green plant fragment has been introduced to the right of P2. The electronic components near P2 have undergone a slight positional shift.
Minimal change observed. The plant in P2 maintains its stable and healthy state. The white cup and its contents remain unchanged. A slight dimming of the overall image is noted.

visual_health_inference.P2_Plant: The plant in P2, from its introduction in T-3 to CURRENT, consistently exhibits signs of good health. Its leaves are dark green, rounded, and show excellent turgor, with no visible signs of wilting, discoloration, or pest damage. The transition from dry soil (EARLIEST, T-5) to moist soil (T-3 onwards) appears to have been a beneficial intervention, supporting its healthy establishment and growth.
visual_health_inference.P_WhiteCup_Fragment: The plant fragment in the white cup (T-1, CURRENT) appears intact and green, suggesting it is either recently introduced or well-preserved in the water. No signs of decay or degradation are visible.

anomalies.description: The expected Mexican Mint plant was absent from P2 in the earliest two observations, with only bare soil present.
The designated scale anchor was not observed in any image throughout the sequence.
The image labeled 'T-4' is completely black, indicating a temporary loss of camera feed or a malfunction.
A pot not listed in the registry was present in the initial images.
Crushed eggshells were introduced onto the soil surface of P2 from T-5 onwards.
A plant was introduced into P2 between T-4 and T-3, replacing bare soil. While consistent with the registry's expectation for P2, its sudden appearance constitutes an intervention.
Various electronic components and wires were introduced onto the desk surface from T-3 onwards.
These items appeared on the desk surface in T-3.

anomalies.item: White plastic cup with water and plant fragment
anomalies.description: This new container and its contents were introduced to the observation area from T-1 onwards.

vision_report.narrative_description: The chronological audit reveals a dynamic environment. Initially, the black pot (P2), designated for Mexican Mint, contained only dry soil, indicating a 'Systemic Loss' of the expected plant. Eggshell fragments were introduced by T-5. A significant event occurred between T-4 (a camera blackout) and T-3, with the sudden appearance of a healthy young plant in P2, inferred to be the Mexican Mint, accompanied by a notable increase in soil moisture. This plant has since shown consistent healthy development, with stable leaf count and turgor through to the 'CURRENT' observation. Concurrently, the observation area saw the introduction of various desk items, including electronic components, and later, a white plastic cup containing water and a plant fragment. The designated 'White Rabbit' scale anchor was notably absent throughout the entire sequence. The plant in P2 appears to be thriving under the current conditions, demonstrating resilience and good health following its introduction and improved soil moisture.
